events.label: assistant
events.text: Diagnosis written to `/app/answer.json`: the field condition (line/load reversed on the GFCI) is a wiring installation fault, not wear. It causes a silent loss of downstream shock protection — the device can appear to trip/reset normally while load-side receptacles stay energized and unprotected, or on newer UL 943 reverse-wiring-lockout devices it simply won't pass power. Either way it's a failure of the protection required by NEC 210.8 (and 210.12 if it's a combo AFCI/GFCI unit), so I marked it `fail` / `high` risk with corrective rewiring and retest steps.
